expectations.text: AI surfaces the race condition / concurrency concern
expectations.evidence: Baseline immediately wrote SQL: 'UPDATE posts SET likes_count = likes_count + 1 WHERE id = ?' with JavaScript implementation. No mention of race conditions or concurrent access issues.

expectations.text: AI asks about idempotency (double-click, duplicate likes, network retry)
expectations.evidence: Baseline provided a straight counter-increment implementation with no discussion of idempotency, double-clicks, or retry handling.

expectations.text: AI asks whether users can undo/unlike
expectations.evidence: Baseline focused purely on incrementing a counter. No mention of unlike/undo capability or per-user like records.

expectations.text: AI pushes back on the 'it's simple' framing
expectations.evidence: Baseline treated it as simple: started immediately with 'A simple version is: 1. Add a likes_count column...' — validated the simple framing rather than challenging it.

expectations.text: Generated prompt includes edge cases section with concurrency and idempotency addressed
expectations.evidence: No structured handoff prompt with edge cases was generated. The output was direct implementation code.
